born 29 July 1928)[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-Silobreaker-3)[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-britannica-4)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-KBE-5)[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-6) is a [Hong Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong") billionaire business magnate, investor, and philanthropist.[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-forbes2024dec-7) He previously served as chairman of [CK Hutchison Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings")[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-8) and [CK Asset Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Asset_Holdings "CK Asset Holdings"), before retiring from those roles in May 2018. Following his retirement, he assumed the position of senior advisor to both companies.[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-9) He is an investor, developer, and operator of the largest health and beauty retailer in Asia and Europe.[\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-Forbes11Richest2008-10) In the March 2024 Forbes list of The Richest People In The World, Li Ka-shing was ranked 38th with a net worth of $37.3 billion.[\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-11) Li has invested in a range of industries, including transportation, real estate, financial services, retail, and energy and utilities.[\[12\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-12) His conglomerate, [Cheung Kong Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Kong_Holdings "Cheung Kong Holdings") has operated businesses across multiple sectors of the Hong Kong economy and has at times accounted for a notable share of the total market capitalisation of the [Hong Kong Stock Exchange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_Stock_Exchange "Hong Kong Stock Exchange").[\[13\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-13) _Forbes Magazine_ and the [Forbes family](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Forbes_family_(publishers) "Forbes family (publishers)") honoured Li Ka-shing with the first ever Malcolm S. Forbes Lifetime Achievement Award on 5 September 2006 in [Singapore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Singapore "Singapore").[\[14\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-14) In spite of his wealth, Li has cultivated a reputation for leading a frugal [no-frills](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/No_frills "No frills") lifestyle, and is known to wear simple black dress shoes and an inexpensive [Seiko](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seiko "Seiko") wristwatch. He lived in the same house for decades, in what has now become one of the most expensive districts in Hong Kong, [Deep Water Bay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_Water_Bay "Deep Water Bay") in [Hong Kong Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_Island "Hong Kong Island"). Li is also a philanthropist, donating billions of dollars to charity and various other philanthropic causes, and owning the [second largest private foundation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka_Shing_Foundation "Li Ka Shing Foundation") in the world after [Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bill_&_Melinda_Gates_Foundation "Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation").[\[15\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-15)[\[16\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-16) In 2019, Forbes put Li in the list of most generous philanthropists outside of the US.[\[17\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-17)[\[18\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-18)[\[19\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-19) ## Early life Li was born in [Chao'an](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chao'an_District "Chao'an District"), [Chaozhou](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chaozhou "Chaozhou") in [Guangdong Province](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guangdong "Guangdong") in 1928 to [Teochew](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teochew_people "Teochew people") parents named Li Yun-ching (1898–1943) and Cheung Bik-chin (1893–1984). Li and his family fled to Hong Kong in 1940 as [refugees](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Refugees_in_Hong_Kong "Refugees in Hong Kong") from the [Sino-Japanese war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_Sino-Japanese_War "Second Sino-Japanese War").[\[20\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-20) Owing to his father's death from [tuberculosis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tuberculosis "Tuberculosis"), he was forced to leave school at the age of 15 and found a job in a plastics trading company where he worked 16 hours a day.[\[21\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-21) In 1950 he started his own company, Cheung Kong Industries.[\[22\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-22) From manufacturing plastics, Li developed his company into a leading real estate investment company in Hong Kong that was listed on the [Hong Kong Stock Exchange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_Stock_Exchange "Hong Kong Stock Exchange") in 1972. Cheung Kong expanded by acquiring [Hutchison Whampoa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutchison_Whampoa "Hutchison Whampoa") and [Hongkong Electric Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hongkong_Electric_Company "Hongkong Electric Company") in 1979 and 1985 respectively.[\[23\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-autogenerated1-23) ## Business career ### Plastics manufacturing In 1950, after learning how to operate a plant, Li founded a plastic manufacturing company in Hong Kong with personal savings and funds borrowed from relatives. Li avidly read trade publications and business news before deciding to supply the world with high quality [plastic flowers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_flower "Artificial flower") at low prices. Li learned the technique of mixing colour with plastics that resemble real flowers. After retooling his shop, he prepared the plant for a visit from a large foreign buyer. Fortunately for Li, the buyer placed a large order and a few years later, Li grew to be the largest supplier of plastic flowers in Asia and made a fortune selling them.[\[24\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-24) ### Real estate In 1958, believing rents would continue to rise, Li decided to purchase a site and develop his own factory building. An opportunity to acquire more land arrived after the [1967 riots](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_1967_Leftist_Riots "Hong Kong 1967 Leftist Riots") when many people fled Hong Kong, and, as a result, property prices plummeted. Li believed the political crisis would be temporary and property prices would eventually rise, and bought land from the fleeing residents at low prices. In 1971, Li officially named his real estate development company [Cheung Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Kong "Cheung Kong") (長江實業). [Cheung Kong Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Kong_Holdings "Cheung Kong Holdings") was publicly listed in [Hong Kong Stock Exchange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_Stock_Exchange "Hong Kong Stock Exchange") in 1972. During board meetings, Li stated on a number of occasions his goal of surpassing the [Jardines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jardines_(company) "Jardines (company)")-owned [Hongkong Land](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hongkong_Land "Hongkong Land") as a leading developer.[\[25\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-25) The successful bid by [Cheung Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Kong "Cheung Kong") for development sites above the Central and Admiralty MTR stations in 1977 was the key to challenging Hongkong Land as the premier property developer in Hong Kong. Despite its size, Jardines decided in the 1980s to protect itself from hostile takeover by Li or other outside investors. The company implemented a cross-shareholding structure that was designed to place control in the hands of Britain's Keswick family despite their less than 10% holdings in the group. In 1984, the company also moved its legal domicile from Hong Kong to another [British overseas territory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_overseas_territory "British overseas territory") – [Bermuda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bermuda "Bermuda"), in anticipation of the transfer of sovereignty of Hong Kong to People's Republic of China in 1997.[\[26\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-26) In an effort to drive forward divestitures of assets in Hong Kong and the Chinese Mainland, Li agreed to sell [The Center](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Center "The Center"), the fifth-tallest skyscraper in Hong Kong.[\[27\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-:0-27) With a value of [HK$](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_dollar "Hong Kong dollar") 40.2billion (US$5.15billion), the deal constitutes the biggest ever office space real estate sale in the Asia-Pacific region.[\[27\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-:0-27) Li sold the Century Link complex in [Shanghai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai") for US$2.95 billion, the second largest transaction for a single building, according to the [Financial Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Financial_Times "Financial Times").[\[28\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-28) In 1979, Li purchased a major stake in [Hutchison Whampoa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutchison_Whampoa "Hutchison Whampoa") from [Hong Kong Bank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HSBC_(Hong_Kong) "HSBC (Hong Kong)") through [Cheung Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Kong "Cheung Kong").[\[29\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-29) ### Retail A subsidiary of CK Hutchison, [AS Watson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AS_Watson "AS Watson") (ASW), is a retail operator with over 15,000 stores. Its portfolio encompasses retail brands in Europe such as [Superdrug](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superdrug "Superdrug") (UK), Marionnaud (France), [Kruidvat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kruidvat "Kruidvat") (Benelux countries), and in Asia including health and beauty retailer [Watson's](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Watson's "Watson's") store and wine cellars et al., PARKnSHOP supermarkets (and spin-off brands), and Fortress electrical appliance stores. ASW also produces and distributes water products and beverages in the region. ### Asset trading [CK Hutchison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings") builds up new businesses and sells them off when shareholder value can be created. Profits were obtained in the sale of its interest in [Orange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orange_SA "Orange SA") to Mannesmann Group in 1999, making a profit of US$15.12billion. In 2006, Li sold 20% of Hutchison's ports business to Singapore rival [PSA Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PSA_Corporation "PSA Corporation"), making a US$3.12billion profit on a US$4billion deal.[\[30\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-30) Group subsidiary [Hutchison Telecommunications](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutchison_Asia_Telecom "Hutchison Asia Telecom") sold a controlling stake of 67% in [Hutchison Essar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vodafone_India "Vodafone India"), a joint venture Mobile operator in India, to [Vodafone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vodafone "Vodafone") for US$11.1billion. ### Internet and technology Li has also made a foray into the technology business, where his investment and venture capital firm Horizons Ventures is specifically allocated towards backing new internet and technology startup firms, and bought a stake in [doubleTwist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DoubleTwist "DoubleTwist").[\[31\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-20090820forbes-31) His other firm, the [Li Ka Shing Foundation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka_Shing_Foundation "Li Ka Shing Foundation") bought a 0.8% stake in social networking website [Facebook](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Facebook "Facebook") for US$120million in two separate rounds,[\[32\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-32)[\[33\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-33) and invested an estimated US$50million in the music streaming service [Spotify](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spotify "Spotify").[\[31\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-20090820forbes-31) Some time between late 2009 and early 2010, Li Ka-shing led a US$15.5million Series B round of financing for [Siri Inc.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siri_Inc. "Siri Inc.")[\[34\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-34) In 2011, Horizons Ventures invested in [Summly](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nick_D'Aloisio#Summly "Nick D'Aloisio"), a website-summarizing app. Notably, the investment made Nick D'Aloisio, Summly's founder, the world's youngest person to receive a venture capital investment at just fifteen years old.[\[35\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-35) In 2012, Horizons Ventures invested US$2.3million in Wibbitz, a company that provides a text-to-video technology that can automatically convert any article post or feed on the web into a video in a matter of seconds. In August 2012, Li acquired a stake in Ginger Software Incorporated.[\[36\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-36) In 2013, Horizons Ventures invested in [bitcoin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bitcoin "Bitcoin") payment company BitPay.[\[37\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-37) In February 2015, Horizons Ventures participated in a US$30 million Series C funding round in Zoom Video Communications.[\[38\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-38) Later in the year, Li participated in a US$108 million Series D round in Impossible Foods.[\[39\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-39) In 2016, he continued investments in technology companies and Horizons Ventures led a US$55million Series A round in [Blockstream](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blockstream "Blockstream"), the leader in blockchain related technologies,[\[40\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-40) and also invested in a startup incubator fund Expa, that works with the founders to build new companies.[\[41\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-41) In September 2017, Li worked with [Alibaba's](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alibaba_Group "Alibaba Group") [Jack Ma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_Ma "Jack Ma") to bring [AlipayHK](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay "Alipay"), a digital wallet service, to [Hong Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong").[\[42\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-42) ### Water Through CK Infrastructure Holdings Limited, Li owns 75% of British water supplier [Northumbrian Water](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northumbrian_Water "Northumbrian Water"), the remaining 25% being held by US private equity firm [KKR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KKR_(company) "KKR (company)"). Despite losses of ~£22 million between March 2022 and July 2023, CK Group and KKR between them took £159 million in dividends from the organisation which in the same period paid its chief executive, [Heidi Mottram](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heidi_Mottram "Heidi Mottram"), $1.24 million. In 2022 the business was fined £240,000 for discharging untreated sewage into a water course in northeast England over a period of two days.[\[43\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-43) ### Australian tax dispute In 2013, a claim was lodged by the [Australian Taxation Office](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australian_Taxation_Office "Australian Taxation Office") (ATO) against [Cheung Kong Infrastructure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Kong_Holdings "Cheung Kong Holdings") (CKI) to pay approximately [A$](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australian_dollar "Australian dollar") 370million in unpaid tax, penalties and interest relating to tax disputes concerning [SA Power Networks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SA_Power_Networks "SA Power Networks") and Victoria Power Networks. The dispute was resolved in 2015 when CKI entered into an agreement with the ATO. No penalty was levied against CKI and a sum of approximately A$24million was refunded from the A$64million previously paid to the ATO by CKI.[\[44\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-44)[\[45\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-45) ### Retirement After his almost-70-year reign over [CK Hutchison Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings") and [CK Asset Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Asset_Holdings "CK Asset Holdings"), Li announced his retirement on 16 March 2018 and the decision to pass control of his US$100billion empire to his son, [Victor Li](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi "Victor Li Tzar-kuoi").[\[46\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-46)[\[47\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-47) He is still involved in the conglomerate as a senior advisor.[\[48\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-48)[\[49\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-49) ### Others Besides business through his flagship companies [CK Asset Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Asset_Holdings "CK Asset Holdings") and [CK Hutchison Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings"), Li Ka-shing has also personally invested extensively in real estate in Singapore and Canada. He was the single largest shareholder of [Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canadian_Imperial_Bank_of_Commerce "Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce") (CIBC), the fifth largest bank in Canada, until the sale of his share in 2005 (with all proceedings donated, see below). He is also the majority shareholder of a major energy company, [Husky Energy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Husky_Energy "Husky Energy"), based in [Alberta](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alberta "Alberta"), Canada.[\[50\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-50) Husky was acquired by Cenovus in 2021, and Li owns 27.2% of the newly merged company. In January 2005, Li announced plans to sell his CA$1.2billion stake in the [Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canadian_Imperial_Bank_of_Commerce "Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce"), with all proceeds going to private charitable foundations established by Li, including the [Li Ka Shing Foundation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka_Shing_Foundation "Li Ka Shing Foundation") in Hong Kong and the Li Ka Shing (Canada) Foundation based in [Toronto](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toronto "Toronto"), Ontario.[\[51\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-51) Li was the non-executive director of the [Hongkong & Shanghai Banking Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HSBC_(Hong_Kong) "HSBC (Hong Kong)") since 1980 and became deputy chairman of the bank in 1985. He was also Deputy Chairman of [HSBC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HSBC "HSBC") in 1991–1992. According to Bloomberg, he had a net worth of US$35.3billion in July 2021.[\[52\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-Bloomberg-52) ## Personal life Li [was married to his first cousin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cousin_marriage "Cousin marriage"), Chong Yuet-ming (1933–1990) in 1962 until her sudden death on [New Year's Day](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Year's_Day "New Year's Day") 1990 due to a suspected drug overdose (other sources name the cause as cardiac arrest).\[ _[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_\] She was the daughter of his maternal uncle (mother's younger brother), Cheung Jing-on (1908–1996) and his wife, Hew Bik-yin (1911–2002), who were also from the [Chaoshan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chaoshan "Chaoshan") region.\[ _[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_\] Both settled in [British Hong Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_Hong_Kong "British Hong Kong") long before Li migrated to the city. He has two sons, [Victor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi "Victor Li Tzar-kuoi") and [Richard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Li "Richard Li"), both Canadian citizens. Victor Li succeeded his father as Chairman of [CK Hutchison Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings") and Chairman of CK Asset Holdings, while Richard Li is Chairman of [PCCW](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PCCW "PCCW"), the largest telecom company in Hong Kong. He is a follower of [Buddhism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buddhism "Buddhism").[\[53\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-53) Li has been called "plainly dressed" for a Hong Kong tycoon. In the 1990s, he wore a HK$50 timepiece from [Citizen Watch](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Citizen_Watch "Citizen Watch") and plain ties. He later wore a [Seiko](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seiko "Seiko").[\[54\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-54) In 2016, he wore a HK$500 Citizen watch.[\[55\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-55) Most recently he has been spotted wearing an [Apple Watch](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Watch "Apple Watch").[\[56\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-56) His son Victor Li was kidnapped in 1996 on his way home after work by gangster [Cheung Tze-keung](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Tze-keung "Cheung Tze-keung") (aka "Big Spender"). Li Ka-shing paid a ransom of HK$1billion directly to Cheung, who had come to his house.[\[57\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-Advice-57) A report was never filed with Hong Kong police. Instead the case was pursued by [mainland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mainland_China "Mainland China") authorities, leading to Cheung's execution in 1998, an outcome not possible under Hong Kong law. Rumours circulated of a deal between Li and the mainland.[\[57\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-Advice-57) ## Awards and honours - [Grand Bauhinia Medal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grand_Bauhinia_Medal "Grand Bauhinia Medal") - [Knight Commander of the Order of the British Empire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Order_of_the_British_Empire "Order of the British Empire")[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-KBE-5) - [Commandeur, Légion d'honneur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/L%C3%A9gion_d'honneur "Légion d'honneur") - Honorary [Doctor of Laws](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Doctor_of_Law "Doctor of Law"), [University of Hong Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Hong_Kong "University of Hong Kong")[\[58\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-58) ## Politics For many years, he was considered to be a pro-Beijing figure. However, in 2010s, tensions had been growing between Li and the mainland. During the [2019–20 Hong Kong protests](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2019%E2%80%9320_Hong_Kong_protests "2019–20 Hong Kong protests"), he refused to outright condemn the protesters, instead urging for peace and calling on the authorities to respond to the protesters humanely.[\[59\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-59) On 4 August 2011 at the interim results announcement for [Hutchison Whampoa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutchison_Whampoa "Hutchison Whampoa"), Li endorsed [Henry Tang](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henry_Tang "Henry Tang") for the [forthcoming chief executive election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012_Hong_Kong_Chief_Executive_election "2012 Hong Kong Chief Executive election").[\[23\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-autogenerated1-23)[\[60\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-60) Then Li said "You all can be just like me, one-person-one-vote (一人一票)."[\[61\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-61) The media then looked at Li in disbelief, and pointed out that regular citizens do not get one-person-one-vote.[\[62\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-62) Li then tried to laugh it off and said "maybe in 2017 they will have one-person-one-vote to choose the chief executive, I probably just said it a little early."[\[23\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-autogenerated1-23)[\[63\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-63)[\[64\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-64) However, Li was criticised by Chinese official state-run press agency [Xinhua](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xinhua "Xinhua") for being ambiguous in his opposition to the [Umbrella movement protests](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2014_Hong_Kong_protests "2014 Hong Kong protests") and his support for [Leung](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leung_Chun-ying "Leung Chun-ying").[\[65\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-20141028wsjecho-65) Later, prior to the Legco vote, Li said that the largest threat to Hong Kong's future was if the government failed to ensure passage of the [2014–15 round of political reform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Hong_Kong_electoral_reform "2014–15 Hong Kong electoral reform").[\[66\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-66) Li's business empire has a presence around the world, including China. Li came under attack from the _[Global Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Global_Times "Global Times")_ newspaper in early 2015, when his companies put out word that it was considering selling prime Shanghai and Beijing properties. It became apparent that Li aimed at re-weighting his asset portfolio to more stable and transparent markets in the West.[\[67\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-20150924ejinsight-67) Concerted attacks ensued and went into a crescendo as China's economy slowed down dramatically in the second half of the year, and the central government sought a way to stem the capital outflows. Specific reproaches were that his asset disposals were "an act of ingratitude" and "immoral at such a sensitive juncture".[\[67\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-20150924ejinsight-67)[\[68\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-nanzao-68)[\[69\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-69) _Security Times_, a _People's Daily_ publication, estimated that Li has sold at least [CN¥](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Renminbi "Renminbi") 73.8billion worth of assets since 2014.[\[67\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-20150924ejinsight-67) Li's holding companies denied divesting in China, saying that its asset disposals were being undertaken in the ordinary course of business.[\[68\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-nanzao-68)[\[70\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-70) The attacks stopped abruptly several weeks later, when editorials in official publications such as _People's Daily_ and _Beijing Youth Daily_ took a neutral stance in unison.[\[67\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-20150924ejinsight-67) ## Charities | | | | --- | --- | | [![icon](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Crystal_Clear_app_kedit.svg/120px-Crystal_Clear_app_kedit.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Crystal_Clear_app_kedit.svg) | This article **may need to be rewritten** to comply with Wikipedia's [quality standards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style "Wikipedia:Manual of Style"). In August 2022, the Li Ka Shing Foundation made a donation of HK$1 million to support Opera Hong Kong to launch the "Li Ka Shing Foundation and Opera Hong Kong Young Artist Overseas Residency Fellowships" to nurture local artists.[\[134\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-134) - In October 2022, the Li Ka Shing Foundation donated HK$3 million to support the Hong Kong Kidney Foundation’s Home Nocturnal Haemodialysis Programme.[\[135\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-135) - In December 2022, the Li Ka Shing Foundation donated HK$30 million to assist Gansu Earthquake relief efforts.[\[136\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-136) - In March 2024, the Li Ka Shing Foundation made a donation of HK$10 million to contribute to the Transcatheter Cardiovascular Interventions Subsidy Scheme (2024-26) of Hong Kong Sanatorium & Hospital.[\[137\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-137) - In May 2024, the Li Ka Shing Foundation donated US$4 million to establish Research Professorship in Hepatology and cancer research funds at the [University of Michigan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Michigan "University of Michigan").[\[138\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-138) - In the second half of 2024, both the LKS Faculty of Medicine of the University of Hong Kong and the Faculty of Medicine of the Chinese University of Hong Kong received an innovative non-invasive cancer treatment device [Histotripsy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Histotripsy "Histotripsy") systems donated by the Li Ka Shing Foundation.[\[139\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-139)[\[140\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-140) - Later, the Foundation also made donations to a number of NGO programmes, namely Precious Blood Hospital (Caritas)’s Cataract Surgery Subsidy Programme (HK$2 million),[\[141\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-141) CUHK Medical Centre’s Urolithotripsy Surgery Subsidy Programme (HK$2 million),[\[142\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-142) Concern Hong Kong’s Oral Health and Dental Care Program (HK$2 million) ),[\[143\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-143) and the TREATS Sport For All Project for students with special needs (SEN) from low-income families (HK$1 million) ).[\[144\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing#cite_note-144) ## See also - - [VodafoneThree](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/VodafoneThree "VodafoneThree") (49%) | | Website | [www.ckh.com.hk](http://www.ckh.com.hk/)[![Edit this at Wikidata](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/8/8a/OOjs_UI_icon_edit-ltr-progressive.svg/20px-OOjs_UI_icon_edit-ltr-progressive.svg.png)](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q19548352#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata") | CK Hutchison Holdings Limited **CK Hutchison Holdings Limited** ( [Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"):長江和記實業有限公司) is a [Hong Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong")–based and [Cayman Islands](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cayman_Islands "Cayman Islands")–registered multinational [conglomerate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conglomerate_(company) "Conglomerate (company)") corporation. The company was formed in March 2015 through the merger of [Cheung Kong Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Kong_Holdings "Cheung Kong Holdings") and its main associate company [Hutchison Whampoa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutchison_Whampoa "Hutchison Whampoa").[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-2) It has four core businesses – ports and related services, retail, infrastructure and telecommunications – which operate in over 50 countries, as well as several other investments around the world.[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-3) ## Holdings The company owns substantial holdings in businesses across a number of industries.[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-4) ### Telecoms The Group's telecommunications division is an operator of [converged telecommunication](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Network_convergence "Network convergence") and digital services around the world.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-AR2021-1) - CK Hutchison Group Telecom – operates [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/3_(company) "3 (company)")-branded [mobile telephone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mobile_telephone "Mobile telephone") networks in [Austria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Austria "Austria"), [Denmark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denmark "Denmark"), [Ireland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Republic_of_Ireland "Republic of Ireland"), [Italy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Italy "Italy"), [Sweden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") and the [United Kingdom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"). - [3 Danmark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/3_(company)#Denmark_and_Sweden "3 (company)") - [Drei Austria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Drei_Austria "Drei Austria") - [Three Ireland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Three_Ireland "Three Ireland") - [Tre Sverige](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/3_(company)#Denmark_and_Sweden "3 (company)") - [VodafoneThree](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/VodafoneThree "VodafoneThree") - [Wind Tre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wind_Tre "Wind Tre") (100% effective interest via CKH) – [Italian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Italy "Italy") operator of [fixed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fixed-line_telephony "Fixed-line telephony") and [mobile telephony](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mobile_telephony "Mobile telephony"), born from the merger between [3 Italy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/3_Italy "3 Italy") and [Wind](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wind_Telecomunicazioni "Wind Telecomunicazioni"). - [Hutchison Asia Telecom Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutchison_Asia_Telecom_Group "Hutchison Asia Telecom Group") – operates [mobile telephone networks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mobile_telephone_network "Mobile telephone network") in [Indonesia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia"), [Sri Lanka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka") and [Vietnam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam"). - [Hutchison Telecommunications Lanka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutch_(Sri_Lanka) "Hutch (Sri Lanka)") - [Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indosat "Indosat") (65,6% via Ooredoo Hutchison Asia, where CKH co-owned its 50% stake with [Ooredoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ooredoo "Ooredoo")) – operates the [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/3_(company)#Indonesia "3 (company)") network in Indonesia alongside IM3. - [Vietnamobile](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vietnamobile "Vietnamobile") – co-owned with Hanoi Telecom. - [Hutchison Telecommunications Hong Kong Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutchison_Telecommunications_Hong_Kong_Holdings "Hutchison Telecommunications Hong Kong Holdings") – operates [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/3_(company) "3 (company)")-branded mobile telephone networks in [Hong Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong") and [Macau](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macau "Macau"). - [3 Hong Kong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/3_Hong_Kong "3 Hong Kong") - [3 Macau](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/3_Macau "3 Macau") - [TPG Telecom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TPG_Telecom "TPG Telecom") (25.05% effective interest, formerly Vodafone Hutchison Australia)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-:0-5) – the second-largest telecommunications company in [Australia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australia "Australia"), owned via the 87.87% CKH-owned Hutchison Telecommunications (Australia) Limited.[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-6) - [Metro Broadcast Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Broadcast_Corporation "Metro Broadcast Corporation") ### Infrastructure The company has investments in energy infrastructure, transportation infrastructure, water infrastructure, waste management, waste-to-energy and household infrastructure. Its investments and operations span across Hong Kong, Mainland China, the United Kingdom, Continental Europe, Australia, New Zealand, Canada and the United States.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-AR2021-1) - [CK Infrastructure Holding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Infrastructure_Holdings "CK Infrastructure Holdings")[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-7)(75.67%) – owns a number of infrastructure companies around the world. - [Eversholt Rail Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eversholt_Rail_Group "Eversholt Rail Group") - [Australian Gas Networks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australian_Gas_Networks "Australian Gas Networks") - [UK Power Networks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UK_Power_Networks "UK Power Networks") - [Northern Gas Networks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northern_Gas_Networks "Northern Gas Networks") - [Northumbrian Water Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northumbrian_Water_Group "Northumbrian Water Group") - [Park 'N Fly Airport Parking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Park_'N_Fly_Airport_Parking "Park 'N Fly Airport Parking") - [Wales & West Utilities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wales_&_West_Utilities "Wales & West Utilities") - [Power Assets Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_Assets_Holdings "Power Assets Holdings") (35.96%)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-:0-5) - [Hongkong Electric Company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hongkong_Electric_Company "Hongkong Electric Company") – Minority 33.37% holding is held through [Power Assets Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_Assets_Holdings "Power Assets Holdings").[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-8) ### Ports As the world's leading port investor, developer and operator, the Group's ports division holds interests in 52 ports comprising 291 operational berths in 27 countries, including container terminals operating in six of the 10 busiest container ports in the world. In 2021, the division handled a total throughput of 88.0 million [twenty-foot equivalent units](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twenty-foot_equivalent_unit "Twenty-foot equivalent unit") ("TEU"). It also engages in river trade, cruise terminal operations and ports related logistic services.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-AR2021-1) - [Hutchison Port Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutchison_Port_Holdings "Hutchison Port Holdings") - Westports Holdings[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-9) (18.84% effective interest)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-:0-5) - Hutchison Port Holdings Trust[\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-10) (30.07%)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-:0-5) In March 2025, it is reported that CK Hutchinson Holding would sell Hutchinson Port Holdings and Hutchinson Port Group Holdings.[\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-11) There are reports stating that the key buyer is the Aponte Italian shipping family, and "the crucial figure" is Diego Aponte, group president of [Mediterranean Shipping Company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mediterranean_Shipping_Company "Mediterranean Shipping Company") (MSC), and chairman of the Terminal Investment Limited (TiL). Gianluigi Aponte, MSC's founder, was reportedly personally playing a role in brokering the potential deal.[\[12\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-12) TiL and [BlackRock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BlackRock "BlackRock") are consortium partners in the port deal.[\[13\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-scmp2025mar-13)[\[14\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-singtao2025mar-14) This comes in the context of U.S. President [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") denouncing Chinese influence over the [Panama Canal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panama_Canal "Panama Canal") and calling for the United States to reclaim control of the canal in some form or another.[\[15\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-15)[\[16\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-16) In July 2025, Panama's government filed two lawsuits against CK Hutchinson's local subsidiary in the country's supreme court.[\[17\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-17) On January 29, 2026, [Panama](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panama "Panama")'s [supreme court](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_court "Supreme court") nullifies the [concession](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concessions_and_leases_in_international_relations "Concessions and leases in international relations") held by [CK Hutchison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings) to operate the [Balboa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Balboa,_Panama "Balboa, Panama") and [Cristóbal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crist%C3%B3bal,_Col%C3%B3n "Cristóbal, Colón") ports at the [Panama Canal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panama_Canal "Panama Canal") as [unconstitutional](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constitution_of_Panama "Constitution of Panama") and orders the facilities to be retendered, following a lawsuit by the [comptroller](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comptroller "Comptroller") after an audit alleged irregularities.[\[18\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-18) These terminals at Balboa and Cristóbal have had their interim operations transferred to MSC and [APM Terminals](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/APM_Terminals "APM Terminals"), a subsidiary of [A.P. Møller – Mærsk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maersk "Maersk"), as of March 11, 2026.[\[19\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-19) The Panamanian government subsequently accused CK Hutchinson of concealing information and obstructing a coordinated transition.[\[20\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings#cite_note-20) ### Retail The Group's retail division is the world's largest international health and beauty retailer, with over 16,300 stores in 28 markets worldwide. Its retail portfolio comprises health and beauty products, supermarkets, as well as consumer electronics and electrical appliances. ##### [Speeches](https://www.lksf.org/category/press-2/speeches/)

# Between Form and Substance

24 February 2006

Minister Mentor Mr. Lee Kuan Yew, Chancellor Dr. Hu, Chairman Mr. Ho, President Professor Hunter, Distinguished Guests, Faculty, Staff and Students:

My warmest congratulations to you on the completion of the new campus and the new library of the Singapore Management University. It will certainly be another bright spot to the vibrancy of Singapore. I wish SMU every success in the years to come.

Today feels a great deal like a fond and endearing walk down memory lane for me. I can still remember vividly the year 1967, and the warm welcome you extended to me and my young family who sought calm in the looming threat of turbulence and uncertainty. I will always recall your kindness with great affection.

I am also happy that our part of the world is very different now than before; we have moved beyond the days when the rhetoric of ideological differences inflamed hatred, caused bloodshed and much heartbreak to its citizens. In Asia we have all benefited from a mass of change, under a new context of “reform and openness”, a wave so forceful in its sweep that it ushered in hope and optimism not only for the core nations but also the world around it.

Yes, we have achieved much but the harsh reality is that the world looks at us not by what we have achieved but by what we will continue to do.

The prime challenge as I see it today is how to nurture responsible citizenship, how to build in the hearts and minds of genuinely progressive young men and women the will as well as the means to carry our true hope for the future. Men and women who are balanced, informed, civil literate, capable of sound judgment and high ideals, who cherish not only the fruits of their labor, or are complacent in their new status as seasoned and opportunistic manufacturers, professionals or entrepreneurs, but the thinking, innovative and inspired men and women who feel a deeper sense of responsibility and public duty towards their world and beyond and care as much for individual dignity as for society’s collective honor.

It would be imprecise and overly simplistic for anyone to say that political structural reform will suffice as the antidote for all the sadness and sense of loss that permeates our history. While we need political institutions that are dependable, honest and built on the rule of law, a civil society must take place in the heart of the individual citizen. It is a commitment that goes far beyond equal participation, individual rights and economic opportunities, but must be attended by a spirit of public duty, a co-responsibility epitomized in the ancient oath of the Citizens of Athens, “We will transmit this city not only, not less, but greater, better and more beautiful than it was transmitted to us.”

If we are to achieve the sustainable dynamism and the flexibility in other successful democracies which enable freedom of choice, breadth of opportunity and wide range of alternatives, yearning only for the democratic mechanism that serves the rights of their citizens is not enough. A flourishing democracy requires the parallel development of the underlying civic habitat that interweaves human mind and spirit, responsibility and reason.

I know it is fashionable to talk about rights, and the mention of personal duty, duty borne by order and virtue, will almost certainly be denigrated with a broad brush, some even consider the mention of duty and responsibility to be anti-democractic. But they could not be further from the truth.

The road to true liberalism is a long one. Freedom and democracy are values cherished by every nation. I love freedom, and I support democracy, but freedom and democracy must be built on a foundation of law and order, the most important cornerstone for sustainable development of any nation. Democracy is a most noble cause thus we must not succumb to the belief that by merely providing the mechanism of participation we have achieved our goal, after all approximated truth is not a species of truth but a species of falsehood; form is important, substance is all the more important; structure might hold us together but substance is the essence of a bright and transcendent future. I believe that this new library structure would find its beautiful soul if it inspires us to seek who we are, who we all are and where we shall be.

Ladies and gentlemen, the experience of age is invaluable, particularly for those who grew up in times of strife, as we can rightfully reflect on the past and be more vocal about our hope for the future. I am not certain whether Minister Mentor agrees with me here but I believe it is not suffering that brings us wisdom, although it will certainly broaden our experience and chasten our pride; in living outside the normal range we may escape from prejudices and received assumptions.

Minister Mentor, you particularly more than the others, you have put your heart and your will to elevate your country from strength to strength, through perplexing times and competitive challenges, unswervingly committed to the principle that progress gained through laying first constructive foundation is the best steadfast manifestation of all that we hope for and all that we hold dear, a true and ordered liberty, a humane and just society, fair and equal participation for all.

It has been a great pleasure and honour to be here. Thank you very much.

Chair of [CK Hutchison Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings") | | Predecessor | [Li Ka-shing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing "Li Ka-shing") | | Spouse | Cynthia Wong | | Parent | [Li Ka-shing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing "Li Ka-shing") | | Relatives | [Richard Li](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Li "Richard Li") (brother) | | Li Tzar-kuoi | | --- | | | | [TraditionalChinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters") | 李澤鉅 | | [SimplifiedChinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters") | 李泽钜 | | | Transcriptions | | --- | | [Standard Mandarin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_Chinese "Standard Chinese") | | [Hanyu Pinyin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanyu_Pinyin "Hanyu Pinyin") | Lǐ Zéjù | | [Yue: Cantonese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cantonese "Cantonese") | | [Jyutping](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jyutping "Jyutping") | Lei5 Zaak6 Geoi6 | | | | **Victor Li Tzar-kuoi** (born 1 August 1964) is a Hong Kong businessman, the chair of the board and group co-managing director of [CK Hutchison Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings") Limited and the chairman of the board and managing director of [CK Asset Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Asset_Holdings "CK Asset Holdings") Limited and the Chairman of [CK Infrastructure Holdings Limited](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Kong_Infrastructure_Holdings "Cheung Kong Infrastructure Holdings"). He is the elder son of tycoon [Li Ka-shing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing "Li Ka-shing") and the brother of [Richard Li](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Li "Richard Li").[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-2) ## Early life and education Victor was born to [Li Ka-shing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing "Li Ka-shing"), a Hong Kong billionaire businessman, at his parents' family house in 79 [Deep Water Bay Road](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_Water_Bay_Road "Deep Water Bay Road"), [Deep Water Bay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_Water_Bay "Deep Water Bay"), Hong Kong on 1 August 1964,[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-zhuanlan.zhihu.com-1)[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-3) He has a younger brother, [Richard Li](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Li "Richard Li"). He attended [St. Paul's Co-educational College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Paul's_Co-educational_College "St. Paul's Co-educational College") in Hong Kong, and holds a [B.S.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bachelor_of_Science "Bachelor of Science") degree in Civil Engineering and a [M.S.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Master_of_Science "Master of Science") degree in Civil Engineering from [Stanford University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stanford_University "Stanford University").[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-0775.HK-4) ## Career On 16 March 2018 his father announced retirement and pass control of his US$100billion empire to his son.[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-5) Victor Li has been holding the following positions:\[ _[when?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")_\] - Chairman and Group Co-managing director of [CK Hutchison Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings") Limited[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-6) - Chairman and managing director of CK Asset Holdings Limited[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-7) - Chairman of CK Infrastructure Holdings Limited[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-8) - Chairman of [CK Life Sciences](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Life_Sciences "CK Life Sciences") Int'l., (Holdings) Inc.[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-0775.HK-4) - Non-executive Director of [Power Assets Holdings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_Assets_Holdings "Power Assets Holdings") Limited[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-9) - Deputy Chairman of HK Electric Investments Limited - Deputy Chairman of Li Ka Shing Foundation Limited, Li Ka Shing (Overseas) Foundation and Li Ka Shing (Canada) Foundation - Co-Chairman of [Husky Energy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Husky_Energy "Husky Energy") Inc.[\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-10) - Director of the [Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hongkong_and_Shanghai_Banking_Corporation "Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation")[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-0775.HK-4) ## Personal life and kidnapping Victor Li was kidnapped in 1996 on his way home after work by notorious gangster ["Big Spender" Cheung Tze-keung](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cheung_Tze-keung "Cheung Tze-keung"). Victor's father, [Li Ka-shing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-shing "Li Ka-shing"), paid a ransom of HK$1.038billion, directly to Cheung who had come to Li Ka-shing's house. Victor Li is said to have been released after one night.[\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-Advice-11) A report was never filed with Hong Kong police. Instead the case was pursued by [Mainland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mainland_China "Mainland China") authorities, leading to Big Spender's execution in 1998, an outcome not possible under Hong Kong law. Rumours circulated of a deal between Li Ka-Shing and the Mainland.[\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-Advice-11) This suggestion, when brought to Li, was brushed off.\[ _[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_\] ## Other positions Victor Li serves as a member of the Standing Committee of the 13th National Committee of the [Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chinese_People's_Political_Consultative_Conference "Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference").[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-0775.HK-4)[\[12\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-12) Li is a member of the Chief Executive's Council of Advisers on Innovation and Strategic Development of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region. Li is the Vice Chairman of the [Hong Kong General Chamber of Commerce](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_General_Chamber_of_Commerce "Hong Kong General Chamber of Commerce"),[\[13\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victor_Li_Tzar-kuoi#cite_note-13) and is [Honorary Consul](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Honorary_Consul "Honorary Consul"), for [Barbados](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barbados "Barbados") in Hong Kong. ## See also - [Li Ka-Shing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li_Ka-Shing "Li Ka-Shing") - [List of kidnappings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_kidnappings "List of kidnappings") ## References 01.
